components.title: SPARC Application
components.description: SPARC (Systematic Policy and Regulatory Controls) is a Rails application for managing NIST 800-53 compliance documentation. This component definition covers authentication and access control implementations.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements account management through the User model with active, suspended, and deactivated status enumerations. Administrative users manage accounts via role assignments. The deactivate! and reactivate! methods enforce account lifecycle transitions. Account status changes are audited via AuditEvent logging.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/user.rb and app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces access control through authorize_admin!, authorize_role!, and authorize_permission! controller concerns. The authorization framework supports 57 granular permission keys mapped to resource types and actions. Unauthorized access attempts are blocked and audited. Phase 2 API controllers (control_catalogs, profile_documents, cdef_documents, control_mappings) enforce Bearer token authentication on all endpoints, with admin-only gates on catalog and mapping write operations.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb, app/models/role.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/control_catalogs_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/profile_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/cdef_documents_controller.rb, and app/controllers/api/v1/control_mappings_controller.rb. Tested by spec/controllers/ and spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces separation of duties through 29 seeded roles: 10 instance-scoped and 19 boundary-scoped. Each role defines a distinct set of permissions preventing any single user from accumulating conflicting privileges. Role assignments are managed by administrators and audited.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/role.rb with role seeds in db/seeds/. Tested by spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements least privilege through granular JSONB permission structures defined in Role::PERMISSION_GROUPS. Each role specifies read/write access per resource type (SSP, SAR, CDEF, catalogs, profiles, users). The authorize_permission! method enforces these constraints on every request. Phase 2 API endpoints enforce admin-only gates for catalog and mapping write operations, while profile and CDEF endpoints are accessible to all authenticated users.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/role.rb (PERMISSION_GROUPS) and app/controllers/concerns/authorization.rb. Phase 2 controllers: app/controllers/api/v1/control_catalogs_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/profile_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/cdef_documents_controller.rb, app/controllers/api/v1/control_mappings_controller.rb. Tested by spec/models/role_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC handles unsuccessful login attempts through session management in the Authentication concern. Failed authentication attempts are logged as audit events with the originating IP address and user agent for monitoring and response.

SPARC displays a configurable login consent banner controlled by the SPARC_BANNER_ENABLED and SPARC_BANNER_MESSAGE environment variables. When enabled, a Bootstrap 5 modal presents the banner content (loaded from file and sanitized for XSS) that users must acknowledge before proceeding to authentication.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/views/sessions/ and app/models/sparc_config.rb. Tested by spec/requests/sessions_spec.rb and spec/system/login_banner_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC implements session lock via configurable idle timeout controlled by the SPARC_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES environment variable (default 60 minutes). The check_session_timeout method in the Authentication concern evaluates session freshness on every request and forces re-authentication when the idle threshold is exceeded.

SPARC provides explicit session termination through the logout action. The end_session method resets the Rails session, clears session cookies, and redirects the user to the login page. All session terminations are recorded as audit events.

SPARC controls permitted actions without identification through the SparcConfig.any_auth_enabled? gate. When no authentication providers are enabled, access is restricted to read-only operations. The configuration is enforced at the controller level before any authorization check.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/sparc_config.rb and app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb. Tested by spec/models/sparc_config_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces remote access protection through FORCE_SSL with HSTS enforcement (1 year max-age, includeSubDomains, preload) in the production environment. All remote connections are encrypted via TLS, and unencrypted HTTP requests are automatically redirected to HTTPS.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in config/environments/production.rb. Tested by deployment verification.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supports multi-factor identification and authentication through multiple configurable providers: local login with bcrypt password hashing, OIDC with SPARC_OIDC_FORCE_MFA enforcement, LDAP directory authentication, and OAuth (GitHub, GitLab) federation. The Authentication concern orchestrates provider selection and credential verification. For API access, SPARC supports 3 mutually exclusive auth modes selected via the SPARC_API_AUTH env var: local (SPARC Bearer tokens only), oidc (OIDC JWT only), and hybrid (OIDC JWT for human users, SPARC Bearer tokens restricted to service accounts with the service_account flag). SessionsController blocks service account web login. Boot-time validation in config/initializers/api_auth.rb ensures correct configuration at startup.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Full IA-2 (including MFA enhancements IA-2(1) and IA-2(2)) requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_FORCE_MFA=true, delegating MFA to the OIDC identity provider. Local-only login (SPARC_ENABLE_LOCAL_LOGIN=true without OIDC) satisfies identification and single-factor authentication but does NOT satisfy MFA requirements. LDAP coverage depends on the directory server's MFA configuration. JWT API authentication requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. Hybrid mode enforces OIDC for humans and SPARC tokens for service accounts only. Recommended production config: enable OIDC with force MFA, disable local login. Implemented in app/controllers/concerns/authentication.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, config/initializers/api_auth.rb, and app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb. Tested by spec/requests/sessions_spec.rb and spec/requests/omniauth_callbacks_spec.rb. See docs/dev/issue_rules.md for the full auth mode coverage matrix.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC uniquely identifies users via email address with case-insensitive uniqueness validation enforced at both the application and database levels. Each user record has a unique database ID and email, preventing duplicate identifiers across the system.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Implemented in app/models/user.rb with database unique index. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC manages authenticator credentials through dual authentication mechanisms: bcrypt password hashing with a minimum length of 12 characters for interactive login, SHA-256 digested SPARC Bearer tokens for API access, and RS256 JWT signature verification for Okta-issued JWTs presented to API endpoints. Password expiry is enforced via the SPARC_PASSWORD_EXPIRY_DAYS configuration (default 30 days), requiring periodic credential rotation. In hybrid mode (SPARC_API_AUTH=hybrid), RS256 JWT is used for human users and SHA-256 token digest for service accounts. Boot-time validation in config/initializers/api_auth.rb ensures correct auth configuration at startup (e.g., OIDC settings present when oidc/hybrid mode selected). JWKS caching uses Rails.cache for multi-process safety.
implemented-requirements.remarks: Full coverage for local login (bcrypt, min 12 chars, expiry). API authenticator management covers both SHA-256 digested SPARC tokens and RS256 JWT signature verification against the OIDC provider's JWKS endpoint. JWT API authentication requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. When OIDC is enabled, password management for federated users is delegated to the OIDC IdP. LDAP password management is delegated to the directory server. Implemented in app/models/user.rb, app/models/api_token.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, and config/initializers/api_auth.rb. Tested by spec/models/user_spec.rb and spec/models/api_token_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC supports non-organizational user identification and authentication through OIDC federation with external identity providers including Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, GitHub, and GitLab. The OmniAuth callbacks controller handles federated identity assertions and maps external identities to local user accounts. External users can also authenticate to API endpoints via Okta-issued JWTs, enabling non-organizational user identification for programmatic access without requiring a local SPARC account with a pre-provisioned API token.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true or OAuth providers enabled. Not met with local-only login (no federation capability). JWT API authentication for external users requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true and SPARC_OIDC_ISSUER_URL to be configured. Implemented in app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb, app/controllers/concerns/api_authentication.rb, and app/models/sparc_config.rb. Tested by spec/requests/omniauth_callbacks_spec.rb.

implemented-requirements.description: SPARC enforces re-authentication by terminating idle sessions after a configurable timeout period (SPARC_SESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES). When the idle threshold is exceeded, the user is redirected to the login page and must re-authenticate to regain access. The timeout is checked on every authenticated request.

SPARC delegates identity proofing to configured OIDC identity providers, which perform identity verification according to their own policies before issuing identity assertions. The application consumes verified identity claims from the provider's token response.
implemented-requirements.remarks: CONDITIONAL COVERAGE: Requires SPARC_ENABLE_OIDC=true. Not met with local-only login (self-registration with no identity proofing). Implemented in app/controllers/omniauth_callbacks_controller.rb. Identity proofing is handled by the external OIDC provider.
